sections.paragraph:
an unused verb having the force of nothing, emptiness, [“commonly”] derived from אַל [“but this is very doubtful”] (which see, and compare the remarks under the root אוּן), whence אֱלִיל vain, powerless.

II. אָלַל to cry out, onomatop. i.q. יָלַל and Arb. أَلَّ Kam. p. 1391. Comp. ἀλαλάζειν. Hence is אַלְלַי.

III. אָלַל kindred to the roots אוּל & אָלָה No. I, prop. to roll, hence to be round, thick; whence אַלָּה, אלֹּון oak, prop. thick tree.
